note: Self-improving loop: improve multicolor pipeline for 20-30 color images; target hw_forest_cat quality.

tried.result: synthetic +3.5%. Forest cat palette analysis at w=0,1,2,4 shows measurable cluster-pick differences but no new color families. Doors likely are painted-wood (brown range), k-means will always cluster with bark regardless of saturation bias. Kept as an OPTION (default=0, no regression) for future use.

observations: 7 synthetic-median param probes rejected; 1 algorithmic extension (saliency-weighted k-means++ init) added as off-by-default option.
Palette analysis across sal=0,1,2,4 on forest_cat: path counts shift 8273→8438→8420→8309, showing init differences. But the color FAMILIES stay stable (same teal/brown/orange/yellow clusters).

Confirmation of iter-3 finding: forest_cat tree doors are probably painted wood in the brown range, not saturated colors that saliency can pull out. k-means on frequency is picking the right thing; the 'washed out' complaint is about insufficient brown/bark gradation, not missing color families.
Counter-intuitive implication: the fix may be simpler than algorithmic — just bumping k higher (50+) for forest cat specifically, since higher k refines the dominant brown range (where doors live).
synthetic oracle cannot guide forest-cat tuning — per-image settings are needed, not global defaults.
recommendation: Loop has exhausted productive single-param tuning. Three forward options: (a) PAUSE and open a slice for per-image opt config (forest_cat uses k=50, default stays k=auto); (b) PAUSE and open a slice for true salient quantization (spatial-outlier detection, not chroma-only); (c) CONTINUE to 10/10 plateau on remaining low-value probes.
